aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.
The Beat Generation was a literary subculture movement started by a group of authors whose work explored and influenced American culture and politics in the post–World War II and Cold War eras.
Irwin Allen Ginsberg (; June 3, 1926 – April 5, 1997) was an American poet and writer.
Brazil, officially the Federative Republic of Brazil, is the largest country in South America.

description: American men's lifestyle and entertainment magazine
description_wiki: Playboy (stylized in all caps) is an American men's lifestyle and entertainment magazine, available both online and in print.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: city in Marin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Sausalito (Spanish for "small willow grove") is a city in Marin County, California, United States, located 1.5 miles (2.4 kilometers) southeast of Marin City, 8 miles (13 km) south-southeast of San Rafael, and about 4 miles (6 km) north of San Francisco from the Golden Gate Bridge.
USCO was an American media art collective in the 1960s, founded by Gerd Stern, Michael Callahan, Steve Durkee, Judi Stern, and Barbara Durkee in New York.

description: largest city in the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Vancouver is a major city in Western Canada, located in the Lower Mainland region of British Columbia.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: private university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, USA, founded 1861
description_wiki: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is a private research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.
Harvard University is a private Ivy League research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

Michael Callahan may refer to:

Michael Callahan (basketball) (fl.
Intermedia Systems Corporation was an American media technology company, co-founded in Cambridge, Massachusetts, in 1969 by Gerd Stern and Michael Callahan.

description: American writer and activist, Jewish Renewal movement pioneer
description_wiki: Meshullam Zalman Schachter-Shalomi (August 17, 1924 – July 3, 2014), commonly called "Reb Zalman" (full Hebrew name: Meshullam Zalman Hiyya ben Shlomo HaCohen), was an American Rabbi, writer, and activist, and one of the founders of the Jewish Renewal movement and an innovator in ecumenical dialogue.
